📊 FinCalc Lab
Calculators Compare Guides About

Financial answers in seconds

113 free calculators for loans, savings, retirement, taxes and debt — each with a plain-English explanation of the math behind the result. No sign-up. Nothing tracked. Everything runs in your browser.

113 calculators 69 in-depth guides 100% free & private

Popular right now

🏠 Mortgage Calculator Full monthly housing cost: loan, property tax and insurance. 📈 Compound Interest Calculator Project how savings grow with monthly deposits and compounding. 🏡 Rent vs Buy a Home Calculator Compare renting vs buying by true net cost over the years you'll stay. 🚗 Lease vs Buy a Car Calculator Compare the true cost of leasing vs buying over the years you keep it. 🏦 15 vs 30 Year Mortgage Calculator Compare payment, total interest, and investing the payment difference. 🔥 FIRE Number Calculator Your target nest egg for financial independence. 🧾 Take-Home Pay Estimator Estimate net monthly pay from gross salary and tax rates. ⚖️ Pay Off Debt vs Invest Calculator Should spare cash attack debt or go into investments? Compare net worth.

All calculators

No calculators match — try another word.

Savings & Investing

📈 Compound Interest Calculator Project how savings grow with monthly deposits and compounding. 🎯 Savings Goal Calculator Find the exact monthly saving needed to hit a target amount. 🌴 Retirement Savings Calculator Project your nest egg and the income it could support. 📊 Investment Return (CAGR) Calculator Turn a total gain into an honest annualized return. 💹 ROI Calculator Total and annualized return on any investment or project. ⏳ Rule of 72 Calculator Estimate doubling time from an interest or growth rate. 🕐 Present Value Calculator Discount a future sum back to today's dollars. 🪙 Dividend Income Calculator Annual dividend income, yield and reinvested growth. 📐 Simple Interest Calculator Interest on principal only, no compounding. 🏦 CD Calculator Maturity value and interest earned on a CD. 🔁 APY Calculator Turn a nominal rate + compounding into true APY. 📉 Real Rate of Return Calculator What your return is really worth after inflation. 💰 Millionaire Calculator Years to reach $1,000,000 (or any target). 🔮 Future Value Calculator Grow a lump sum plus deposits to a future value. 🪤 Investment Fee Impact Calculator How much a fund's annual fee costs you over time. ☕ Latte Factor Calculator What a small recurring spend could grow to if invested. 🎓 College Savings Calculator Projected education cost and the monthly saving to fund it. ⏱️ Cost of Waiting to Invest Calculator How much delaying investing by a few years really costs. ⏳ Time to Save Calculator How long a monthly amount takes to reach a target. 🎯 Required Return Calculator The annual return needed to reach a target on time. 🪜 CD Ladder Calculator Split a lump sum into a CD ladder: rung schedule, blended APY, and what each year unlocks.

Retirement

🏦 401(k) Calculator Project 401(k) growth including the employer match. 💰 Roth IRA Calculator Project tax-free Roth IRA growth from regular contributions. 🏖️ Retirement Withdrawal Calculator How long a nest egg lasts at a given withdrawal. 💵 Annuity Payout Calculator Monthly income a lump sum can pay over a period. 🔥 FIRE Number Calculator Your target nest egg for financial independence. 🏄 Coast FIRE Calculator The amount that grows to retirement with no new savings. 🏛 Social Security Break-Even Calculator Compare claiming at 62, full retirement age or 70 — and the age each choice pays off. ⚖️ Roth vs Traditional 401(k) Calculator Compare after-tax retirement income from pre-tax vs Roth contributions. 🩹 HSA Calculator Project your HSA and see the triple tax advantage vs a 401(k) or taxable account. 📅 RMD Calculator Your required minimum distribution by birth year, plus a year-by-year projection. 🔄 Roth Conversion Calculator Convert at today's tax rate vs staying traditional — final spendable wealth, both ways. 🚨 401(k) Early Withdrawal Calculator Taxes, the 10% penalty, what actually lands in your account — and what the money would have become. 🏛️ Pension Lump Sum vs Annuity Calculator The return your lump sum must earn to match the pension checks — the one number that decides.

Loans & Debt

💳 Loan Payment Calculator Monthly payment, total interest and cost of any amortized loan. 🏠 Mortgage Calculator Full monthly housing cost: loan, property tax and insurance. 🚀 Loan Payoff Calculator See how extra monthly payments shorten a loan and cut interest. 🧊 Debt Avalanche vs Snowball Calculator Compare the two classic payoff strategies on your own debts. 🚗 Car Affordability Calculator Turn a monthly budget into a realistic car price. 💳 Credit Card Payoff Calculator How long to clear a card, and what interest costs you. ⚖️ Debt-to-Income (DTI) Calculator The ratio lenders use to approve mortgages and loans. 🔑 House Down Payment Calculator Down payment amount and monthly saving to reach it. 🔄 Mortgage Refinance Calculator Monthly savings and break-even point of refinancing. 🎓 Student Loan Calculator Monthly payment and total interest on student debt. 🗝️ Car Lease Calculator Estimate a monthly lease payment from the key terms. 🏡 Extra Mortgage Payment Calculator How extra payments cut mortgage interest and time. 🗓️ Biweekly Mortgage Calculator How biweekly payments shorten your mortgage. 📅 Amortization Schedule Calculator Yearly principal vs interest breakdown of any loan. 🧮 Debt Consolidation Calculator See if one consolidated loan beats your current debts. 🏦 Home Equity Calculator Your home equity and borrowable amount at a target LTV. 🏡 Home Affordability Calculator Max home price from your income using the 28/36 rule. 📍 Mortgage Points Calculator Whether paying points to lower your rate pays off. ⚖️ Loan Comparison Calculator Put two loan offers side by side to see the real cost. 🏡 Home Sale Proceeds Calculator Your net cash after paying off the mortgage and selling costs. 🧵 Blended Debt Interest Rate Calculator Weighted-average interest rate across all your debts. 🏦 How Much Can I Borrow Calculator Max loan a monthly payment can support. 💳 Credit Utilization Calculator Your credit utilization ratio and its score impact. 🚗 Lease vs Buy a Car Calculator Compare the true cost of leasing vs buying over the years you keep it. 🏡 Rent vs Buy a Home Calculator Compare renting vs buying by true net cost over the years you'll stay. ⚖️ Pay Off Debt vs Invest Calculator Should spare cash attack debt or go into investments? Compare net worth. 🏦 15 vs 30 Year Mortgage Calculator Compare payment, total interest, and investing the payment difference. 🚗 Auto Loan Calculator Monthly payment with trade-in value, sales tax, fees and any rolled-in loan balance. 💳 Balance Transfer Calculator 0% promo vs staying on your current card — fee included, with the payment that clears it in time. 🏦 HELOC Calculator How big a credit line your equity supports, and payments in both phases — including the jump. ⚖️ ARM vs Fixed Mortgage Calculator 5/7/10-year ARM vs 30-year fixed over the years you'll actually keep the loan — with the cap-out worst case. 🚪 PMI Calculator Your monthly PMI, total cost, and the three dates it can end — including the appreciation shortcut. 🔑 Closing Costs Calculator Itemized buyer closing costs and total cash to close — lender, title, escrow and prepaids. 📉 Mortgage Recast Calculator What a lump sum + re-amortization does to your payment — vs just prepaying, vs refinancing. 🎓 Student Loan Refinance Calculator New rate vs old: monthly and lifetime savings — and the federal protections a refi permanently burns.

Income & Budgeting

💰 Salary to Hourly Calculator Convert annual salary to hourly, weekly and monthly pay. 🧾 Take-Home Pay Estimator Estimate net monthly pay from gross salary and tax rates. 💸 50/30/20 Budget Calculator Split your take-home pay into needs, wants and savings. 🛟 Emergency Fund Calculator Size your emergency fund and see how long it takes to build. 🎈 Inflation Calculator See what today's money will be worth in the future. 📋 Net Worth Calculator Add up what you own, subtract what you owe. 📆 Hourly to Salary Calculator Convert an hourly wage into weekly, monthly and yearly pay. 🏡 Rent Affordability Calculator A sensible rent range based on your income. ⏰ Overtime Pay Calculator Total weekly pay including overtime at any multiplier. 💵 Paycheck Calculator Gross and net pay per paycheck by pay frequency. 📈 Pay Raise Calculator New pay after a raise, and if it beats inflation. 💹 Savings Rate Calculator Your savings rate and what it means for freedom. 📈 Rent Increase Calculator New rent after an increase, and if it's fair. 📉 Salary Inflation Calculator Whether your salary has kept pace with inflation. 🚗 Commute Cost Calculator Yearly cost of commuting, including the value of your time. 🛩️ Savings Runway Calculator Months your savings would cover with no income. 💰 Salary Gross-Up Calculator The gross salary needed to net a target take-home. 💰 Bonus Tax Calculator Bonus withholding (22% federal + FICA + state) and what you truly owe at tax time. 🛡️ Life Insurance Needs Calculator Coverage sized from your debts, income, kids and savings — not a salesperson's rule of thumb. 👶 Daycare vs Staying Home Calculator Second paycheck minus childcare, taxes and work costs — the honest hourly wage that's left. 📦 RSU Tax Calculator Vest-day tax at your real bracket vs the 22% withheld — and the April surprise in between.

Taxes & Shopping

🧾 Sales Tax Calculator Add sales tax to a price, or back it out of a total. 🏷️ Discount Calculator Final price after a percentage off, and what you save. 🍽️ Tip Calculator Work out the tip and split a bill between people. 🧾 VAT Calculator Add VAT to a net price or extract it from a gross price. 📈 Capital Gains Tax Calculator Estimate tax owed and net profit on an investment sale. 🧾 Tax Bracket Calculator (2025) Your marginal bracket, effective rate and tax bill under the 2025 federal brackets. 🗓️ Quarterly Estimated Tax Calculator Your four payment amounts and due dates — safe-harbor prior-year method vs 90% of this year. 🧾 Tax Withholding Calculator Project your year-end refund or bill from a pay stub, and the exact W-4 line that fixes it. 🏠 Property Tax Calculator Estimate the annual bill and monthly escrow from your home value and state — or your exact local rate.

Business

⚖️ Break-Even Point Calculator How many sales cover your costs — the first business question. 📉 Profit Margin Calculator Margin, markup and profit from cost and price. 🧑‍💻 Freelance Hourly Rate Calculator Turn a target income into the hourly rate you must charge. 🏷️ Markup Calculator Set a selling price from cost and desired markup. 🎯 LTV : CAC Ratio Calculator Lifetime value vs acquisition cost — is growth healthy? 🚀 Startup Runway Calculator Months of cash left from burn rate and balance. ⏳ Payback Period Calculator Time for an investment to recoup its cost. 📐 EBITDA Calculator EBITDA and margin from your income statement. 🏘️ Rental Property ROI Calculator Cap rate, cash flow and cash-on-cash return. 🧑‍💻 Self-Employment Tax Calculator SE tax, the half-deduction, income tax estimate and your quarterly payment. 🧰 Solo 401(k) vs SEP IRA Calculator Max contributions to each from your self-employment profit — and why the solo 401(k) usually wins.

Everyday Math

🔢 Percentage Calculator Three everyday percentage questions in one tool. ⛽ Fuel Cost Calculator Cost of a trip, and yearly fuel spend. 🛒 Unit Price Calculator Find the real cost per unit and the better deal.

Guides & explainers

"What Is a 401(k) Match, and Why It's the Best Deal in Finance" How employer 401(k) matching works, why skipping it means leaving free money on the table, and how vesting and contribution limits affect you. "Changing Jobs? The Four Things You Can Do With Your Old 401(k)" Leave it, roll it to the new plan, roll it to an IRA, or cash out — the real trade-offs of each, the direct-rollover rule that avoids a tax disaster, and who should pick which. "The 50/30/20 Rule in Real Life: Where It Works, Where It Breaks, How to Fix It" An honest field guide to the most famous budgeting rule — including what to do when rent alone eats 45% of your income. "APR vs APY: The Difference That Costs (or Earns) You Money" APR and APY look alike but mean different things. Understand compounding, why lenders quote APR and banks quote APY, and how to compare fairly. "How to Build Wealth in Your 20s and 30s" The moves that matter most when you're young — why starting early beats everything, and a simple playbook that turns modest income into long-term wealth. "Buy Now, Pay Later: Convenience or Debt Trap?" How buy-now-pay-later really works, why 'interest-free' installments can still hurt your finances, and how to use BNPL without falling into the trap. "The Dealer Finance Office: Seven Traps That Cost Car Buyers Thousands" The profit center of a car dealership isn't the car — it's the finance office. Payment packing, rate markup, negative equity roll-ins, and the add-on gauntlet, explained with the math. How Compound Interest Builds Wealth (and Why Starting Late Costs So Much) A plain-English walkthrough of compound growth, with real numbers showing why time in the market beats timing — and what starting five years late actually costs. "How Credit Card Interest Really Works (and How to Pay Zero)" How credit card APR, daily compounding and the grace period actually work — why balances snowball, and the simple habit that lets you pay no interest at all. "What Actually Affects Your Credit Score" The five factors behind your credit score, which ones matter most, and the practical habits that raise it — without the myths. "Debt-to-Income Ratio: The Number Lenders Care About Most" What your debt-to-income ratio is, why lenders use it to decide loans and mortgages, the thresholds that matter, and how to improve yours. "Diversification: Why You Shouldn't Bet Everything on One Thing" What diversification is, why it's called the only free lunch in investing, and how to spread risk across companies, asset types and geographies the simple way. "Do You Need Life Insurance? Term vs Whole Life, Explained" Who actually needs life insurance, how much, and why term insurance beats whole life for most people. A clear guide to a confusing, heavily-sold product. "Dollar-Cost Averaging: Why Boring, Automatic Investing Wins" How dollar-cost averaging works, what the research actually says versus lump-sum investing, and why the biggest benefit is behavioral, not mathematical. "How Big Should Your Emergency Fund Be? (And Where to Keep It)" How to size your emergency fund for your situation, where to keep it so it's safe but accessible, and how to build one without derailing other goals. "Emergency Fund vs Investing: Which Dollar Goes Where First?" A decision framework for the classic dilemma — cash safety versus market growth — including the order of operations most planners actually recommend. "Estate Planning Basics: Why Everyone Needs a Plan (Not Just the Rich)" What estate planning really involves — wills, beneficiaries, powers of attorney — why it matters at any wealth level, and the simple steps to protect your family. "ETFs vs Mutual Funds: Which Should You Choose?" The real differences between ETFs and mutual funds — trading, fees, taxes and minimums — and why for most long-term investors it barely matters which you pick. "The Financial Order of Operations: What to Do With Your Money First" A clear, prioritized sequence for every spare dollar — emergency fund, employer match, high-interest debt, investing — so you never wonder what comes next. "The FIRE Movement Explained: Financial Independence by the Numbers" What FIRE really means, the 25x rule and 4% withdrawal, the variants (Lean, Fat, Coast, Barista FIRE), and the one number that decides your timeline. "First-Time Home Buyer Guide: From Saving to Closing" A step-by-step guide for first-time buyers — how much to save, what you can afford, the full costs, and the process from pre-approval to getting the keys. "The Freelancer Tax Survival Guide: SE Tax, Quarterlies, and the Deductions That Matter" Everything self-employment does to your taxes in one place — the 15.3% SE tax, quarterly payment schedules, the deductions worth real money, and the retirement accounts that shelter profit. "Good Debt vs Bad Debt: A Simple Framework for Borrowing Decisions" Not all debt is equal. A practical framework for telling wealth-building debt from wealth-destroying debt, using interest rate, asset value and cash flow. "How Much House Can You Afford? The 28/36 Rule and Its Honest Limits" A practical framework for setting your housing budget — the 28/36 rule, the costs first-time buyers forget, and when the guidelines mislead. "How Much Should You Save for Retirement?" Rules of thumb, the real math behind retirement targets, and how to turn a scary abstract goal into a monthly number you can actually act on. "How Much Should You Spend on a Car? Rules of Thumb That Actually Work" Practical guidelines for a sensible car budget — the 20/4/10 rule, why total cost dwarfs the sticker price, and how a car quietly drains wealth. "How to Build Credit From Scratch (and Fix a Low Score)" A practical, step-by-step guide to building a credit history from zero and raising a low score — what actually moves the needle and what to ignore. "How to Handle a Market Crash Without Wrecking Your Finances" What to do — and what never to do — when markets fall. Why crashes are normal, why selling locks in losses, and how to turn a downturn to your advantage. "How to Make a Budget That Actually Sticks" A practical guide to budgeting that survives real life — the main methods compared, how to set one up in an afternoon, and why most budgets fail. "How to Negotiate a Raise (Without the Awkwardness)" A practical, evidence-based guide to asking for a raise — how to build your case, what number to name, the words to use, and why it compounds over a career. "How to Prepare for a Recession (Before It Arrives)" Practical steps to recession-proof your finances — strengthen your safety net, income, and debt position now, and why panic-selling investments is the wrong move. "Index Funds Explained: Why the Boring Choice Beats the Pros" What an index fund is, why it quietly outperforms most active managers after fees, and how to use one as the core of a simple, low-cost portfolio. "10 Investing Myths That Keep People Poor" The most common investing myths — from 'you need to be rich to start' to 'timing the market works' — debunked with the evidence, so you can invest with confidence. "Lease vs Buy a Car: The Honest Math (and When Each Wins)" A clear framework for deciding whether to lease or buy your next car — what leasing really costs, why buying usually wins long-term, and the cases where leasing makes sense. "Lifestyle Creep: Why Earning More Doesn't Make You Richer" How lifestyle inflation quietly eats every raise, why high earners can still live paycheck to paycheck, and a simple rule to keep more of what you earn. "How to Manage Money as a Couple (Without Fighting About It)" Practical approaches to combining finances with a partner — joint vs separate accounts, handling different incomes, and the conversations that prevent money fights. "Marginal vs Effective Tax Rate: The Misunderstanding That Costs People Real Money" Why "being in the 24% bracket" doesn't mean losing 24% of your income, how progressive brackets actually work, and the raise myth debunked with numbers. "A Money Checklist for Every Decade of Life (20s to 60s)" The key financial moves for each stage of life — from building habits in your 20s to preparing for retirement in your 60s — in one clear roadmap. "Why Net Worth Is the Only Number That Really Matters" Income tells you how fast you're rowing; net worth tells you where the boat actually is. How to calculate it, track it, and use it to build wealth. "Opportunity Cost: The Hidden Price Tag on Every Money Decision" The most important concept in economics, applied to your money — why every dollar and hour has a hidden cost, and how thinking in trade-offs makes you richer. "Passive Income: What Actually Works vs. the Hype" An honest look at passive income — why most 'passive' income isn't passive, which approaches genuinely build hands-off cash flow, and the scams to avoid. "How to Pay Off Debt Faster: A Strategy Guide That Respects Both Math and Psychology" Avalanche, snowball, consolidation and balance transfers compared honestly — which debt payoff strategy fits your numbers and your temperament. "Should You Pay Off Your Mortgage Early? The Honest Answer" The math and the psychology of paying off a mortgage early versus investing — when each wins, and why the 'right' answer is partly emotional. "How to Price Freelance Work Without Underselling Yourself" Why new freelancers charge too little, how to calculate a rate that actually sustains you, and when to move from hourly to value-based pricing. "The Psychology of Money: Behavioral Biases That Quietly Cost You" Money decisions are emotional, not just mathematical. The most common behavioral biases that sabotage investors and savers — and how to defend against them. "Rental Property Investing Basics: The Numbers That Matter" A beginner's guide to judging a rental property — cap rate, cash flow, cash-on-cash return, the expenses rookies forget, and where the real returns come from. "Renting vs Buying a Home: The Honest Math" Beyond 'rent is throwing money away' — the real costs on both sides, the break-even horizon, and how to decide based on your actual situation. "RMDs: How Required Minimum Distributions Work — and How to Shrink Them" At 73 or 75 the IRS starts forcing money out of your pre-tax accounts. How RMDs are calculated, the 25% penalty, and the three legal ways to defuse a large one before it arrives. "RMDs and the Roth Conversion Window: Defusing Retirement's Tax Time Bomb" Big pre-tax balances turn into forced taxable income at 73 or 75. How RMDs snowball, why the years just after retirement are prime conversion season, and how bracket-filling works in practice. "Roth vs Traditional: One Question Decides It (Plus Three Tiebreakers)" The Roth vs traditional choice comes down to your tax rate now versus in retirement. How the math really works, why Roth wins more often than the formula suggests, and what to do in the murky middle. "Roth vs Traditional: Which Retirement Account Wins?" A clear framework for choosing between Roth and Traditional retirement accounts — the tax logic, the break-even rule, and why many people use both. "How to Save for a House Down Payment (Without It Taking Forever)" How much to save for a down payment, where to keep it, and practical tactics to get there faster — plus why 20% isn't always the right target. "Saving vs Investing: What's the Difference and When to Do Each" Saving and investing are not the same thing. What sets them apart, why you need both, and a simple rule for deciding which to do with any given dollar. "Sequence of Returns Risk: Why When You Retire Matters as Much as How Much" The retirement danger hiding inside average returns — why a crash early in retirement is far worse than the same crash later, and how to defend against it. "How to Set Financial Goals You'll Actually Achieve" Why vague money goals fail and specific ones succeed — how to set SMART financial goals, prioritize them, and turn each into an automatic monthly number. "Sinking Funds: The Simple Trick for Big Expenses" How sinking funds turn irregular, budget-wrecking expenses into small predictable monthly savings — and why they beat relying on your emergency fund. "How to Start Investing With Little Money: A Beginner's Roadmap" You don't need thousands to start investing. A practical, jargon-free roadmap for beginners — what to do first, what to buy, and the mistakes to skip. "Stocks vs Bonds: How to Think About Asset Allocation" What stocks and bonds actually are, how they behave differently, and how to choose a stock/bond mix that matches your timeline and stomach for risk. "How to Stop Living Paycheck to Paycheck" A practical, judgment-free plan to break the paycheck-to-paycheck cycle — even on a modest income — by creating breathing room and building it into a buffer. "How to Survive a Job Loss Financially" A calm, practical action plan for losing your job — what to do in the first days, how to stretch your money, and how to protect your long-term finances. "How to Teach Kids About Money (by Age)" Age-by-age ideas for raising money-smart kids — from piggy banks to first jobs and investing — building the habits that schools rarely teach. "The Time Value of Money: Why a Dollar Today Beats a Dollar Tomorrow" The single most important idea in finance, explained plainly — present value, future value, discounting, and why it underpins every investment and loan decision. "The True Cost of Owning a Car (It's Not the Payment)" Depreciation, insurance, fuel, maintenance and financing — the full cost of car ownership, and how to spend far less on getting around. "Understanding Inflation: How It Quietly Shrinks Your Money" What inflation is, why central banks target it, how it erodes cash and helps borrowers, and how to protect your money from losing value over time. "Understanding Your Paycheck: Where Your Money Actually Goes" A line-by-line guide to your pay stub — gross vs net, taxes, and deductions — so you know exactly why your take-home is less than your salary. "Value-Based Spending: Frugality Without Feeling Deprived" How to spend lavishly on what you love and cut hard on what you don't — a sustainable alternative to restrictive budgeting that actually makes you happier. "What to Do With a Windfall: Bonus, Inheritance, or Lump Sum" A calm, step-by-step plan for handling a sudden sum of money — from a bonus to an inheritance — so it builds lasting wealth instead of vanishing. "When to Claim Social Security: The 62 vs 67 vs 70 Decision, Honestly" Claiming age changes your Social Security check by up to 77% for life. How the reductions and credits work, what break-even really tells you, and who should claim early anyway. "When Does Refinancing Your Mortgage Make Sense?" How to tell whether refinancing will save you money — the break-even rule, the costs people forget, and the traps that make a lower rate a worse deal.

Why FinCalc Lab?

Private by design

Every calculation happens in your browser. Your salary, debts and savings never leave your device.

The math, explained

Each tool shows the formula it uses and walks through what the result actually means for you.

No paywalls, ever

Free to use, no accounts, no "premium" upsell. Supported by unobtrusive advertising.

FinCalc Lab

Free, fast and honest financial calculators. All tools run entirely in your browser — we never see or store your numbers.

About Methodology Contact Privacy Policy Terms of Use

© 2026 FinCalc Lab. All calculators and articles are for educational purposes only and do not constitute financial advice. Consult a qualified professional before making financial decisions.
